reforms.description: The Government has initiated a multi-annual investment programme aimed at improving infrastructure and supporting the start-up of new industries, with a growing emphasis on research and development (R&D).
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The investment programme is designed to enhance R&D capabilities and foster new industries, which are expected to contribute positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been initiated and is currently in force.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant structural change in the government's approach to R&D funding and infrastructure development.
reforms.source_quote: The Government has given priority to maintaining the attractiveness of Luxembourg as a site for industrial activities with a multi-annual investment programme aimed at improving the infrastructure and supporting the start-up of new industries, with a growing emphasis on research and development (R&D).

reforms.description: The Government announced company tax cuts to encourage foreign investment in Luxembourg and preserve the competitiveness of the economy, including the abolition of the local working capital tax and a gradual reduction of the corporate income tax.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The tax cuts are aimed at attracting foreign investment, which is expected to enhance economic competitiveness and stimulate growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The tax cuts have been officially announced but are not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a major shift in tax policy aimed at fostering a more attractive investment climate.
reforms.source_quote: The Government announced company tax cuts – equivalent to LF 4.1 billion in 1997 – to encourage foreign investment in Luxembourg and preserve the competitiveness of the economy.
